ops.body:
# getaway v2 rulings: preferences never gate; budgeted expansion

Supersedes "Getaway skill redesign: balance-bias, seat quality ranking, transfer-first affordability" (98be9f3). The balance-bias posture generalized in v2 from a rule about balances into the engine's core law. Updated 2026-07-14 for the quality-restoration waves (a6b5efaa "feat(gates)"): the confirmed-constraint and veto slots of the hard set are now concretely wired.

## Preferences never gate — feasibility, confirmed constraints, and vetoes may

The complete hard set: structural journey validity (ordered, non-overlapping segments; return departs after outbound arrives), known seat insufficiency (`seat_sufficiency: insufficient`, judged on the live-expanded `/trips/{id}` row — never the cached `/search` teaser; `unknown` stays visible with a verification warning), endpoint vetoes, the `avoid_transit` veto over real transit points (award-leg connections, both sides of every boundary; landside self-transfer points; cash-hop connection airports — never the origin, final destination, or round-trip turnaround), hard-avoid carriers on any expanded segment, and explicitly confirmed `constraints`, each wired and disclosed per journey in `dropped`: `mileage_limit`, `cabin` (any award segment booking below the constrained cabin drops, compared per segment; cash legs exempt — bridge quotes are economy by construction), and `departure_days` (the outbound's first-departure weekday). Everything else — dates, trip length, departure weekdays, cabin, mileage target, balances, elite status, seat verdicts — orders or annotates through CLI-computed fit facts and tier verdicts, never removes. A declared `preferences.<key>.priority` folds into factor tiers deterministically (it changes ordering strength, never gates; see the ranking-lanes ruling). A journey beyond the preferred window composes and carries its named miss; the renderer, not the model, guarantees `preference_misses` display.

## Soft means swept wider, not filtered later

Soft dates sweep the stated range plus `SOFT_DATE_SEARCH_PADDING_DAYS` (7) — padding independent of preference strength; confirmed-hard dates sweep exact bounds. All cabins ride one call; `include_filtered=true` so the server can't hide expensive near-misses; sources are never trimmed to preferences. A miss must exist in the cache before the model can judge it. `plan.origins` omitted defaults from prefs `origin_airports` (else `[home_airport]`) at read time, and the prefs fingerprint covers the source keys so an origin edit invalidates swept artifacts.

## Expansion is budgeted per endpoint, disclosed when it truncates

`EXPANSION_BUDGET_PER_ENDPOINT` / `RETURN_EXPANSION_BUDGET_PER_ENDPOINT` (12 each), selection cohort-diversified over (endpoint, date, program, cost), truncation always disclosed. No global cap; one hot endpoint can't starve the others. `PRESENTATION_LIMIT` (6) applies only after assess; notable preference stretches from beyond the cut come from assess picks plus the deterministic per-code trigger (see the ranking-lanes ruling).

## Surviving rulings, carried forward unchanged

- **`barely` demotes in-band**: a `barely` seat verdict soft-demotes within its cost tier, keeps its spot, and carries an explicit warning; unknown products rank neutral.
- **QAF first for Africa**: Africa sweeps lead with the undocumented `QAF` pseudo-code; per-program continent fallbacks sweep all programs funded-first, never trimmed.
- **Pseudo-codes never reach filters** (new, from the v1 zero-finalists bug): `WST`/`QAF` are sweep-time operands the server expands; every feasibility check compares the expanded concrete airports recorded on the leg.
